programmes.show_description: Glen devours Harri's fast food foul-up, Alice is a busy bee working on Susan's foot, Sketch knocks out boxer Russell's tattoo, and Jay tackles a badger bodge-up.
Ardal O'Hanlon straddles a mannequin, Chris Ramsey takes a sausage to the nose, Sophie Duker asks a very good question, and Alex Horne displays a mastery of mimicry.
Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy game show mash-up. Roisin Conaty and Kiri Pritchard-McLean take on Jon Richardson and Rob Beckett. With Huge Davies, Susie Dent and Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Aberdeen. Singer Lek and professional footballer Josh's Asian and West Indian menu competes with Ange and Davie's controversial kangaroo curry and Jennifer and Jason's travel-inspired food.
In Aberdeen, Jen and Callum go all-out American, while their rivals Debbie and Shaun, and Sam and Jamie, are shocked to discover that they're serving near-identical Scottish menus.
The One in a Hot Air Balloon: The sky really is the limit for David as he plans nuptials in a hot air balloon for bride-to-be Elle. How will she take it?
The One in a Car Park: In an attempt to avoid tradition, will James forget he's planning a wedding? And will his urban vision for his and Hollie's big day be bold or leave his bride cold?
The Wacky West Country One: What will bride Alex, who loves Pride and Prejudice, make of pig racing and her groom, Mark, in dungarees and wellies on their big day?
The Bungee One: Will bride-to-be Ellie, who hates flying, go along with her groom Craig's heart-stopping ideas?
The Midsummer Night's Wedding One: James longs for a traditional big day, so how will he react when groom Jack books a cast of actors to perform a Shakespeare classic?
CEO Andy Wirth takes on a new identity to work incognito among his own employees at a gigantic ski resort near Lake Tahoe.
Fatburger, based in Los Angeles, is known as the 'last great hamburger stand'. CEO Andy Wiederhorn dons a disguise to go undercover in his own organisation.
PJ's Steakhouse: In New York, Gordon Ramsay visits PJ's Steakhouse, where owners Joe and Madalyn are in serious debt and struggling to keep the high-end restaurant afloat.
Fleming: Gordon Ramsay visits Fleming, a Danish restaurant in Miami. Once the best family-run restaurant in town, it's now losing money fast.
Sushi Ko: Akira and Lisa are losing up to $20,000 a month at their sushi restaurant in California. Can Gordon piece the family back together and save the restaurant?
Lido Di Manhattan: Ambitious graduate Lisa bought her restaurant in California at just 23 but, five years later, she's worried she picked the wrong venture. Can Gordon help?
Anna Vincenzo's: Gordon Ramsay visits Italian restaurant Anna Vincenzo's in Florida, where owner CeCe has lost her way in the kitchen. But can she take constructive criticism?
Jimmy Carr hosts as Rhod Gilbert and Sarah Millican go head-to-head with Judi Love and Joe Lycett. Jazz Emu joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner.
Jimmy Carr, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host the anarchic game show. Rob Beckett and Joe Wilkinson take on Sarah Millican and Jamali Maddix. With Glenn Moore.

programmes.show_name: Jordan Gray: Is It a Bird?
programmes.show_description: Jordan brings her comedy to TV for the first time. Shot in her Essex home-town theatre, this stand-up special is a whirlwind of music and comedy covering everything from politics to Batman.
Guests Louie Spence, Rachel Riley, David O'Doherty and Joe Lycett join host Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson for the topical events game show.
John Hannah, Example, Holly Walsh and Bobby Mair join host Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson for this edition of the topical panel game.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Rhod Gilbert and Sarah Millican go head-to-head with Judi Love and Joe Lycett. Jazz Emu joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping

programmes.show_name: Don't Tell the Bride: Let It Snow
programmes.show_description: Steve from Rotherham plans a wedding with an icy twist for his bride Emma, who wants an impressive, upmarket affair. But will his big ideas leave him snowed under?
The One with the Wannabe DJ Groom: Bride Ellis has dreamed her whole life of a quiet church wedding. Luke decides to plan a rave-inspired do...
The One on Top of a Mountain: Richard's gambling everything on a Welsh mountain-top wedding for his bride Jody, but even his best man is unsure about his plans.
John Wilson, the president and chief operating officer of Rollins Incorporated - the parent company of eight pest control firms - goes incognito to work among his employees.
Randy DeWitt, the CEO of sports bar and grill chain Twin Peaks, goes incognito among his own staff, to find out if the company's culture is keeping up with its rapid growth.
In west London, Queen and Shawn take inspiration from their Thai and West Indian heritage. Paul and Suzy plan a fancy French feast. And Charlotte and Kevin serve up Japanese cuisine.
On the south coast, there are experimental flavour combinations, banjo playing and a Billy Connolly lookalike, as three rival couples put their hosting skills to the test.
In Southampton, Roxi and Glen's guests are dreading their cauliflower purée. But Roxi and Glen think John and Suzy's fennel-stuffed salmon is the stuff of nightmares.
Mama Rita's: Gordon tries to bring new ideas to Mama Rita's Mexican restaurant in Newbury Park, southern California.
Le Bistro: Gordon visits high-end Le Bistro in Florida. Owner Andy has cooked for top chefs in the past, but he's lost his passion - and he doesn't respond well to criticism.
Gordon Returns 1: Gordon revisits three restaurants - Handlebar, Casa Roma and The Black Pearl - to check in with the owners he helped. Has his advice been put to good use?
Gordon Returns 2: Gordon Ramsay revisits two restaurants - Giuseppi's and Sante La Brea - to find out whether his advice has been put to good use.
Spanish Pavillion: Gordon Ramsay visits Spanish Pavillion, a family-run restaurant whose owners, brothers Michael and Jerry, spend more time struggling for power than working.
Taskmaster's back! Amy Gledhill, Armando Iannucci, Joanna Page, Joel Dommett and Kumail Nanjiani leave all dignity at the door as they hunt for worms and feed Greg his five-a-day.
Jimmy Carr presents as Sarah Millican and Josh Widdicombe take on Alan Carr and Thanyia Moore. The Delightful Sausage join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.

programmes.show_name: Rhod Gilbert: The Book of John
programmes.show_description: Raw, personal and brutally honest, award-winning comedian Rhod takes us on a no-nonsense journey into finding humour when you've hit rock-bottom. Recorded at the Wales Millennium Centre.
Louis Smith, Tony Law, Alice Levine and Carol Vorderman join host Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson for this edition of the topical panel game.
